category_title: Medium-proficiency international students struggling with sentence structure and translation
minimal_context: The student is reviewing the second draft of their essay to correct run-on sentences and repeated words.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's review the second draft of your essay and work on correcting the run-on sentences and repeated words.

STUDENT: When you revised my essay I understood all my errors and those were mostly the run-on and repeated sentences. So, I have to work a lot to improve my essay.

TUTOR: What do you think you should do next to fix them?

STUDENT: Writing was very hard as I imagined. Since I am not a good talker, I had troubles with expressing my thoughts in a proper way.

TUTOR: Just keep trying to rewrite the sentences.

STUDENT: But after the revision of my second draft, I found more errors and they were the same, but this time it was run-on sentences and repeated words. I found it hard because there were errors in almost all my essay and I was difficult for me to correct my essay. Because of the problems with use of English language, I lost confidence in writing.

options.A: Employ elements of trauma-informed care to the writing process, understanding how trauma impacts lives and applying that understanding to the design of the essay assignments.
options.B: Conduct careful pre-intervention functional assessments to identify relationships between the writing environment and the student's behavior during the essay revision process.
options.C: Provide scheduled work breaks during the essay revision process, making these breaks available to the student for free rather than contingent upon their writing performance.
options.D: Require the student to participate in a sequenced writing project, writing a total of four papers on the same subject over the course of the semester.
